must_fix.title: BQ duplicate rows on re-sync
must_fix.problem: WRITE_APPEND disposition for line_items, features, and predictions means re-runs or scheduler retries produce duplicate rows in BigQuery. PostgreSQL is protected by ON CONFLICT; BigQuery has no equivalent on append.
must_fix.fix: Option A: Switch to MERGE after loading to a staging table, deduplicating on (id) for predictions, (report_id, feature_definition_id, computation_version) for features. Option B: Use partition-level WRITE_TRUNCATE scoped to the run's fiscal_year values. Option C: At minimum, scope the export queries by prediction_run_id = run_id instead of WHERE TRUE to limit exposure.

must_fix.title: Non-atomic DELETE + COPY operations
must_fix.problem: autocommit=True means DELETE FROM financial_line_items and the subsequent COPY FROM are separate transactions. A crash between them loses data with no recovery path.
must_fix.fix: Wrap each DELETE+COPY pair in an explicit transaction: pipeline_conn.raw.autocommit = False; try: DELETE; COPY; conn.raw.commit(); finally: conn.raw.autocommit = True. Apply to both etl_batch._copy_line_items and feature_compute.compute_features_for_reports.

must_fix.title: BQ export queries are unscoped — export entire tables
must_fix.problem: line_items, features, and predictions queries use WHERE TRUE with an optional KRS filter. This exports the entire latest view on every run rather than just the current run's data. Combined with WRITE_APPEND, this guarantees duplicates after the second run.
must_fix.fix: Filter predictions by prediction_run_id = run_id (already available as parameter). Filter line_items and features by report_id IN (SELECT report_id FROM predictions WHERE prediction_run_id = run_id). This scopes each export to only new data.

should_fix.title: GCS staging bucket has no lifecycle policy
should_fix.problem: Parquet files accumulate at line_items/run={run_id}/data.parquet paths. After a year of daily runs: ~1500 files. No cleanup mechanism exists.
should_fix.fix: Add a 7-day lifecycle rule: gsutil lifecycle set '{"rule":[{"action":{"type":"Delete"},"condition":{"age":7}}]}' gs://rdf-pipeline-staging. Document this in PIPELINE_GCP_SETUP.md Phase 0.

should_fix.title: export_training_data hardcodes Maczynska features only
should_fix.problem: The training data export pivot hardcodes x1_maczynska through x6_maczynska. Poznanski features (x1-x4_poznanski) are missing. The feature_set_id parameter is accepted but never used in the query. PostgreSQL equivalent in app/services/training_data.py dynamically pivots by feature_set.
should_fix.fix: Query feature_set_members for the requested feature_set_id to get the list of feature IDs, then dynamically build MAX(IF(cf.feature_definition_id='...', cf.value, NULL)) AS ... clauses in the SQL. Alternatively, add a second hardcoded block for Poznanski features as a quick fix.

should_fix.title: compute_population_stats scans all partitions
should_fix.problem: The CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE query joins predictions and companies with no partition filter on fiscal_year. Tables are range-partitioned by fiscal_year but this query scans all partitions. At scale (10M+ predictions), each scan costs ~$5/TB.
should_fix.fix: Add WHERE p.fiscal_year >= EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE()) - 5 to prune partitions if historical scores are not needed for population stats. Or parameterize the lookback window.

should_fix.title: BigQuery IAM is project-scoped, not dataset-scoped
should_fix.problem: Pipeline service account has roles/bigquery.dataEditor at project level. This grants write access to any dataset in the project, not just rdf_analytics.
should_fix.fix: Scope to dataset: bq add-iam-policy-binding --role=roles/bigquery.dataEditor rdf-api-project:rdf_analytics --member=serviceAccount:pipeline-runner@rdf-api-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com. Keep roles/bigquery.jobUser at project level (required for running queries).

nice_to_have.title: Row-by-row queue inserts in enqueue_changed_since
nice_to_have.problem: Loops over discovered rows and calls enqueue_krs() one at a time. For large backlogs (thousands of new docs), this issues thousands of individual INSERT statements.
nice_to_have.fix: Use psycopg2.extras.execute_values with the ON CONFLICT clause to batch all inserts into a single round-trip. Or use a temp table + INSERT INTO ... SELECT pattern.

nice_to_have.title: GCS storage.Client created per upload call
nice_to_have.problem: _upload_to_gcs creates a new storage.Client() on every call. For 4 uploads per run this is negligible, but it instantiates credentials, HTTP sessions, and metadata lookups each time.
nice_to_have.fix: Create the client once in sync_run() and pass it as a parameter to _upload_to_gcs. Signature becomes _upload_to_gcs(storage_client, bucket, path, blob).

nice_to_have.title: Missing report metadata columns in BQ export
nice_to_have.problem: PG financial_reports has data_source_id, report_type, logical_key, and ingestion_status. None are exported to BQ. Analytics queries that need to filter by report type or data source cannot do so.
nice_to_have.fix: Add data_source_id STRING and report_type STRING to bq_schema.FINANCIAL_LINE_ITEMS (or create a separate financial_reports table in BQ). Update the li_sql query to include fr.data_source_id and fr.report_type.

nice_to_have.title: BQ classification column type may diverge
nice_to_have.problem: BQ schema defines classification as INT64. Current discriminant models return integer classifications (0/1/2/3). If a future ML model returns string classifications, the parquet export will fail or type-coerce silently.
nice_to_have.fix: Change classification to STRING in bq_schema.py to match the PG column type (which accepts any value). Cast to string in the export query: p.classification::text AS classification.

nice_to_have.title: Population stats tenure bucket uses DATE_DIFF not age()
nice_to_have.problem: DATE_DIFF(CURRENT_DATE(), incorporation_date, YEAR) counts calendar year boundaries, not complete years. A company incorporated Dec 31 2024 shows tenure=2 on Jan 1 2026 (2 boundaries crossed) vs 1 complete year. Not a divergence since this only runs in BQ, but could surprise analysts.
nice_to_have.fix: Use DATE_DIFF(CURRENT_DATE(), incorporation_date, DAY) / 365 for approximate complete years, or document the boundary-counting behavior in the query comment.

optimizations.action: GCS lifecycle 7-day TTL on staging bucket
BQ partition pruning in compute_population_stats (fiscal_year filter)
optimizations.savings_usd: prevents cost spikes at scale (>$5/scan at 10M+ rows)

optimizations.action: Consider BQ flat-rate slots if analytics scans exceed $20/month

scaling_thresholds.under_10k_reports: Current single Cloud Run Job works fine
scaling_thresholds.10k_to_100k_reports: Filter BQ export by prediction_run_id to avoid full-table exports
scaling_thresholds.100k_to_1m_reports: Parallel Cloud Run Jobs (queue supports SKIP LOCKED) + MERGE for BQ dedup
scaling_thresholds.over_1m_reports: Chunked processing, consider Dataflow for BQ load, read replicas for Cloud SQL

parity_status.json_columns: consistent — explicit ::text cast, tested in parity suite
parity_status.training_data_export: INCONSISTENT — BQ hardcodes Maczynska only, PG is dynamic
parity_status.tenure_calculation: acceptable — BQ-only computation, no PG equivalent to diverge from
